coreCompetencies: Ability to read electronic schematics and identify components
Elementary knowledge of multimeter and bench power supply use
Ability to solder through-hole and basic surface-mount components
Basic understanding of passive components (resistors, capacitors, inductors)
Elementary knowledge of PCB layers and copper traces
Ability to assemble and populate prototype boards with guidance
Understanding of datasheets and component parameters
Basic knowledge of Ohm's law and simple circuit analysis
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with a schematic capture tool (KiCad, Altium)
Basic knowledge of electrostatic discharge handling and lab safety
Elementary understanding of digital and analog signals
indicators: Assists in board assembly and basic measurements

coreCompetencies: Ability to design schematics for simple circuits (power regulation, sensor interface)
Ability to lay out two-layer PCBs with basic routing
Practical knowledge of component selection against electrical parameters and availability
Understanding of oscilloscope and logic analyzer measurements
Ability to perform board bring-up and power-on checks
Knowledge of decoupling and bypass capacitor placement
Understanding of voltage regulator selection (linear, switching)
Ability to document a bill of materials with sourcing information
complementaryCompetencies: Basic knowledge of signal integrity concepts
Familiarity with reflow and rework stations
Understanding of board-level interface standards (USB, I2C, SPI)

coreCompetencies: Mastery of multi-layer PCB layout with controlled impedance
Ability to design power distribution networks and regulation stages
Ability to debug boards with oscilloscope, logic analyzer and thermal camera
Deep knowledge of component derating and tolerance analysis
Ability to design for signal integrity (length matching, differential pairs)
Understanding of EMC principles (shielding, grounding, filtering)
Ability to plan and execute board bring-up and performance characterization
Knowledge of DFM rules for assembly and fabrication
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of thermal simulation and heatsink sizing
Ability to design test points and debug interfaces
Understanding of battery and power management circuits

coreCompetencies: Ability to architect complex mixed-signal boards
Mastery of high-speed digital design (DDR, high-speed serial links)
Ability to design power sequencing and management for multi-rail systems
Deep knowledge of EMC pre-compliance testing and mitigation
Ability to lead design for test (DFT) with boundary scan and in-circuit test
Mastery of thermal management design (airflow, thermal vias, heat spreaders)
Ability to drive schematic and layout reviews
Knowledge of certification requirements (CE, FCC, EMC) and test planning
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of RF circuit design fundamentals
Ability to design for automated optical inspection
Understanding of reliability testing (thermal cycling, HALT)

coreCompetencies: Expertise in complex board and system-level electronic design
Ability to design hardware platforms and reference designs
Mastery of signal and power integrity simulation and analysis
Ability to lead pre-compliance and certification campaigns (CE, FCC, EMC)
Deep knowledge of design for manufacturing and cost optimization
Ability to define hardware validation strategy and test coverage
Expertise in component qualification and second-source strategy
Mastery of manufacturing collaboration and design handoff
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of high-voltage or power electronics design
Ability to design for high-reliability applications (automotive, industrial)
Understanding of supply chain and component lifecycle management

coreCompetencies: Ability to define hardware architecture strategy for product lines
Expertise in system-level electronic design across board interactions
Mastery of hardware reliability and qualification programs
Ability to lead new product introduction with contract manufacturers
Deep knowledge of certification strategy across regions and standards
Ability to define component and supplier strategy
Expertise in design for manufacturing at production scale
Mastery of hardware cost engineering and BOM optimization
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of failure analysis and root cause methods
Ability to define hardware test infrastructure (fixtures, ATE)
Understanding of regulatory strategy for new markets
indicators: Directs hardware design for product lines

coreCompetencies: Technical leadership in hardware engineering across an organization
Ability to define electronic design standards and design rules
Expertise in hardware platform strategy across product families
Mastery of certification and regulatory strategy for global markets
Ability to define hardware reliability and quality targets
Deep knowledge of manufacturing economics and design-to-cost
Expertise in building hardware engineering teams and capabilities
Ability to define component and supply chain strategy
complementaryCompetencies: Ability to establish hardware design review governance
Ability to define test and validation infrastructure strategy
Knowledge of hardware intellectual property and patents

coreCompetencies: Strategic vision of hardware technology evolution
Ability to lead hardware architecture across a product portfolio
Expertise in hardware due diligence for acquisitions and technology evaluation
Mastery of manufacturing and supply chain strategy for hardware
Ability to define hardware reliability and certification excellence
Deep knowledge of design-to-cost and hardware economics at scale
Expertise in aligning hardware decisions with product strategy
Ability to establish hardware engineering standards adopted across the organization
complementaryCompetencies: Ability to shape component supplier partnerships
Ability to define the hardware technology roadmap
Knowledge of emerging electronic technologies and processes

coreCompetencies: Executive leadership in hardware engineering strategy
Ability to align the hardware roadmap with product and business strategy
Expertise in managing hardware development and manufacturing budgets
Mastery of hardware portfolio and platform strategy
Ability to build hardware engineering organizations
Deep knowledge of hardware supply chain and manufacturing risk
Expertise in hardware certification and regulatory strategy globally
Ability to drive hardware innovation across product lines
complementaryCompetencies: Ability to shape strategic manufacturing and supplier partnerships
Ability to influence hardware standards in the industry
Knowledge of hardware technology investment
indicators: Defines and executes hardware engineering vision
Accountable for hardware development and manufacturing
